dashboard.label: Authentication Overview
dashboard.description: Monitor authentication events across the domain. Track failed logons, lockouts, and suspicious authentication patterns. | ATT&CK: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1110 (Brute Force)

search.query: index=wineventlog EventCode IN (4624, 4625) earliest=-24h | stats count by ComputerName | sort - count
index=wineventlog EventCode=4625 ComputerName=$src_host$ TargetUserName=$target_account$ | stats count as total_failures
index=wineventlog EventCode=4624 ComputerName=$src_host$ TargetUserName=$target_account$ | stats count as total_success
index=wineventlog EventCode=4740 ComputerName=$src_host$ | stats count as lockout_count
index=wineventlog EventCode=4625 ComputerName=$src_host$ | stats dc(TargetUserName) as unique_accounts
index=wineventlog EventCode=4625 ComputerName=$src_host$ TargetUserName=$target_account$ | timechart span=1h count as "Failed Logins" by TargetUserName limit=10 useother=false
index=wineventlog EventCode=4625 ComputerName=$src_host$ TargetUserName=$target_account$ | stats count as failure_count by TargetUserName | sort - failure_count | head 10

index=wineventlog EventCode IN (4624, 4625) ComputerName=$src_host$ | eval LogonTypeDesc=case( LogonType=2, "2 - Interactive", LogonType=3, "3 - Network", LogonType=4, "4 - Batch", LogonType=5, "5 - Service", LogonType=7, "7 - Unlock", LogonType=8, "8 - NetworkCleartext", LogonType=9, "9 - NewCredentials", LogonType=10, "10 - RemoteInteractive (RDP)", LogonType=11, "11 - CachedInteractive", 1=1, LogonType." - Unknown" ) | stats count by LogonTypeDesc | sort - count

panel.title: Successful Logins After Multiple Failures (Possible Brute-Force Success)

search.query: index=wineventlog EventCode IN (4624, 4625) ComputerName=$src_host$ TargetUserName=$target_account$ | eval status=if(EventCode=4624, "Success", "Failure") | stats count(eval(status="Failure")) as failures count(eval(status="Success")) as successes earliest(eval(if(status="Success", _time, null()))) as first_success latest(eval(if(status="Failure", _time, null()))) as last_failure values(IpAddress) as source_ips values(LogonType) as logon_types by TargetUserName, ComputerName | where failures >= 5 AND successes > 0 | eval first_success=strftime(first_success, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") | eval last_failure=strftime(last_failure, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") | eval risk=case( failures >= 20 AND successes > 0, "CRITICAL", failures >= 10 AND successes > 0, "HIGH", failures >= 5 AND successes > 0, "MEDIUM", 1=1, "LOW" ) | sort - failures | table TargetUserName, ComputerName, failures, successes, source_ips, logon_types, last_failure, first_success, risk | rename TargetUserName as "Account", ComputerName as "Host", failures as "Failed Attempts", successes as "Successful Logins", source_ips as "Source IPs", logon_types as "Logon Types", last_failure as "Last Failure", first_success as "First Success", risk as "Risk Level"

index=wineventlog EventCode=4740 ComputerName=$src_host$ | eval lockout_time=strftime(_time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") | stats count as lockout_count earliest(_time) as first_lockout latest(_time) as last_lockout values(TargetDomainName) as domain by TargetUserName, ComputerName | eval first_lockout=strftime(first_lockout, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") | eval last_lockout=strftime(last_lockout, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") | sort - lockout_count | table TargetUserName, domain, ComputerName, lockout_count, first_lockout, last_lockout | rename TargetUserName as "Locked Account", domain as "Domain", ComputerName as "DC Reporting", lockout_count as "Lockout Events", first_lockout as "First Lockout", last_lockout as "Last Lockout"
